interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a notable hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y peaks at 12.68%. The 2Y anomaly likely reflects acute near-term liquidity stress in the banking sector and persistent inflation expectations above BB's 10% repo rate. The 364D dip below 182D suggests segmented demand in T-bill auctions. The 130bps 10Y-91D spread signals moderate term premium compressed by tight BB monetary policy and elevated CRR-driven liquidity constraints.
implications: The 2Y bulge indicates banking sector stress and expectations that BB may maintain or tighten the repo rate beyond 10% in the near term, reflecting sticky inflation above target
Segmented and illiquid secondary markets are distorting price discovery, particularly in the 1-5Y segment, suggesting institutional demand-supply mismatches from banks' SLR-driven buying
The steep long-end (15Y-20Y) signals fiscal sustainability concerns and elevated government borrowing expectations, implying crowding-out risks for private sector credit
